leagueBriefing: The heavy hitters survived a day of scares at Indian Wells, as Novak Djokovic and Carlos Alcaraz both dropped sets but advanced, while Alex Michelsen pulled off the shock of the tournament by ousting Taylor Fritz.

games.headline: Djokovic shakes off rust to survive Kovacevic scare in Indian Wells opener
games.brief: Novak Djokovic's return to the desert was anything but a walk in the park. The world number one looked uncharacteristically vulnerable in the second set against Aleksandar Kovacevic, but his trademark mental toughness surfaced in the decider to avoid a massive opening-round upset.
bullets: Djokovic dominated the opening set 6-4 before losing focus and dropping the second 1-6.
Kovacevic broke the legend twice in the second set, playing some of the best tennis of his career.
The top seed cleaned up his unforced errors in the third set to seal the 6-4, 1-6, 6-4 victory.
This marked Djokovic's first competitive match in several weeks, showing clear signs of competitive rust.
games.context: Djokovic moves on to face Jack Draper in a highly anticipated second-round clash.

games.headline: Michelsen stuns Fritz in all-American upset at Indian Wells
games.brief: In a battle of generations for American tennis, 21-year-old Alex Michelsen proved he’s ready for the big stage by taking down the former champion Taylor Fritz. Michelsen played fearless tennis in the pressure moments, particularly during a tense second-set tiebreak, to record the biggest win of his young career.
bullets: Michelsen broke Fritz early in the first set and never looked back, taking it 6-4.
Fritz struggled with his first-serve percentage, allowing Michelsen to dictate play from the baseline.
The second set went to a tiebreak where Michelsen rallied from 4-6 down to win 8-6.
This is Fritz's earliest exit at Indian Wells since 2018.
games.context: The loss is a significant blow to Fritz's ranking points as he was defending deep-run points from previous years.

games.headline: Alcaraz overcomes tiebreak blip to overpower Rinderknech
games.brief: Carlos Alcaraz looked like he was in for a short night until a loose tiebreak handed Arthur Rinderknech a lifeline. However, the Spaniard's explosive power proved too much in the final set as he sprinted to the finish line, reminding the crowd why he is a perennial favorite in the desert.
bullets: Alcaraz lost a tight second-set tiebreak 7-8 (6-8) after holding a mini-break lead.
The Spaniard responded by winning the first four games of the third set in just 15 minutes.
Rinderknech hammered 12 aces but was broken four times during the match.
Alcaraz finished with 34 winners compared to just 18 unforced errors.
games.context: Alcaraz keeps his hopes of reclaiming the world No. 1 spot alive with the win.

games.headline: Medvedev delivers clinical masterclass to dismiss Baez
games.brief: While others struggled, Daniil Medvedev was the picture of efficiency. The 'Chess Player' dismantled Sebastian Baez's clay-court style with relentless depth and a near-perfect second set, spending less than 80 minutes on court to secure his spot in the next round.
bullets: Medvedev won a staggering 92% of his first-serve points during the match.
The second set was a total lockout, with Medvedev taking it 6-0 in just 24 minutes.
Baez was unable to generate a single break point against the Medvedev serve.
This win marks Medvedev's 10th consecutive victory on hard courts this season.
games.context: Medvedev's form makes him a heavy favorite for the title given the struggles of other top seeds.

games.headline: Norrie grinds down De Minaur in tactical desert battle
games.brief: Cameron Norrie out-dueled one of the fastest players on tour by simply refusing to miss. In a match defined by long rallies and tactical patience, the former champion used his flat backhand to neutralize De Minaur's speed and claim a straight-sets victory.
bullets: Norrie saved all three break points he faced in the first set.
De Minaur committed 25 unforced errors, frustrated by Norrie's defensive consistency.
Both sets ended 6-4, mirroring the tight nature of their head-to-head history.
Norrie has now reached the third round in Indian Wells for five consecutive years.
games.context: Norrie's win sets up a clash with Rinky Hijikata in the next round.

games.headline: Hijikata outlasts Bublik's trickery in three-set marathon
games.brief: Rinky Hijikata proved that substance beats style at Indian Wells. Despite Alexander Bublik's usual array of underarm serves and tweeners, the young Australian stayed disciplined through two tiebreaks to secure a gritty comeback win that left the crowd exhausted.
bullets: Bublik won the opening set tiebreak 7-3 but lost focus in the middle of the match.
The match featured three sets that all went to at least 6-3 or a tiebreak.
Hijikata won the final set tiebreak 7-3 to pull off the upset.
Bublik served 15 aces but also 11 double faults in a wildly inconsistent performance.
games.context: Hijikata earns his first ever third-round appearance at a Masters 1000 event.

games.headline: Draper dominates Cerundolo to set up blockbuster Djokovic date
games.brief: Jack Draper is playing with a level of confidence that should put the rest of the draw on notice. He made quick work of the seeded Francisco Cerundolo, using his heavy lefty forehand to dictate play and ensure he didn't spend any more time in the heat than necessary.
bullets: Draper broke Cerundolo in the very first game of the match and never trailed.
The Brit won 80% of his second-serve return points in a dominant first set (6-1).
Cerundolo showed more fight in the second set (7-5) but was undone by Draper's late break.
Draper hit 22 winners to only 9 unforced errors in a very clean display.
games.context: Draper's reward is a second-round match against world No. 1 Novak Djokovic.

games.headline: Ruud fights back to deny Vacherot a fairy-tale upset
games.brief: Casper Ruud narrowly escaped what would have been the surprise of the day. The qualifier Valentin Vacherot played the set of his life to take the opener, but Ruud’s superior fitness and heavy topspin eventually wore down the underdog in the desert heat.
bullets: Vacherot stunned the world No. 8 by taking the first set 6-3 with aggressive net play.
Ruud adjusted his return position in the second set, winning it 6-3 to force a decider.
The third set was a battle of nerves, with Ruud securing the decisive break at 4-4.
This was Vacherot's first ever match against a top-10 opponent.
games.context: Ruud advances to a clash with Carlos Alcaraz in what could be the match of the tournament.
